comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Refined via design brainstorm 2026-06-17. Scope narrowed to a lossy NDJSON interop projection (reporting/data-mining, primary) + clean rebar-to-rebar migration (secondary). Backup dropped (git bundle of the tickets branch already covers it). MCP export/import tools dropped (CLI/library only). Added: provider-neutral --strip-external seam, provenance fields (source_id/source_created_at/source_author/source_env), idempotent skip by source_id, STATUS reproduction of non-open states. Performance: streaming export via reduce_ticket + deferred-push import (REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=off); batch-commit primitive deferred to a follow-up task. GitHub Issues sync filed as a separate follow-on epic (rides the reconciler, not import).

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
Decomposed into 4 independently-measurable, individually-green sub-tasks (each leaves the tree green: existing + new tests pass):
- T1 fd2b-b26e-9fef-4bae — Provenance plumbing (composer+reducer+state+schema). Foundation. No blockers.
- T2 3121-87f8-f3a0-4cf1 — NDJSON export (CLI+lib+export.schema.json+strip-external). No blockers.
- T3 e249-1034-3237-44b0 — NDJSON import core (two-pass, STATUS reproduction, dry-run). depends_on T1+T2.
- T4 25d0-13ba-7f9e-4615 — Import idempotency (skip by source_id) + deferred-push perf + docs. depends_on T3.
Ordering via depends_on links; file_impact set on each for next_batch. Ready now: T1, T2.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
COMPLETE — implemented across 4 green sub-tasks (each left the tree green; final full suite 2191 passed / 9 skipped / 0 failures, ruff clean).

AC coverage:
- [x] NDJSON export (streaming via reduce_ticket) + per-line schema_version + scope flags + provider-neutral --strip-external/--no-jira + schemas/export.schema.json — T2 (minty-index-match)
- [x] import composes events via locked path (CREATE+STATUS+COMMENT/LINK/TAG), two-pass, --dry-run — T3 (jaunty-phase-dunce)
- [x] provenance fields (source_id/source_created_at/source_author/source_env) carried + surfaced in ticket_state; STATUS reproduction of non-open states — T1 (curt-vigor-spade) + T3
- [x] idempotent skip by source_id (streaming target scan); existing tickets never updated — T4 (brawny-hub-musk)
- [x] import defers push (REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=off) + single final push; large-import limitation documented (docs/import-export.md) — T4
- [x] src/rebar/_io/ adapters: export_ndjson, import_ndjson, _provenance, _strip (+ _cli) — note: NDJSON names per refined design, not the original export_json/import_json
- [x] tests: round-trip≡logical state; idempotent re-run (0 dups); strip-external; status reproduction; dangling-link skip; provenance surfacing; streaming-memory sanity
- [x] DROPPED: MCP export_tickets/import_tickets (+outputSchema) — CLI/library only, as designed

Commits: 4d5cb230 (T1), 6119c24d (T2), 6f701958 (T3), 3e694a8a (T4).

compiled_state.description:
# P1.2 — rebar export / import (NDJSON interop projection + clean rebar→rebar migration)

## Context (problem + research)

rebar has no export/import; git history is the only "export". Refined via a design brainstorm (2026-06-17). The purpose is a **lossy interop projection, NOT a backup** — `git bundle create tickets.bundle tickets` already gives a lossless, full-audit-trail backup, so a reduced-state JSON export would be strictly worse for that. Two consumers:

- **Primary — reporting / data-mining.** A flat, tool-friendly export other systems can parse (DuckDB / jq / pandas / BigQuery).
- **Secondary — clean rebar→rebar migration.** Export a store, import into a different repo cleanly, with an option to omit external-tracker (Jira) associations so tickets re-map into a new project.

Prior art: git-issue dumps the DB as JSON and keeps a checkpoint/SHA map for idempotent re-import; git-bug tags bugs with origin and treats bridges as bidirectional+incremental. Foreign-tracker **sync** (GitHub parity with Jira) is explicitly OUT of scope here — it belongs on the existing reconciler framework (binding store + bidirectional differs + applier), not on one-shot import, which is stateless/additive/lossy and would have to rebuild the entire binding/diff/conflict layer. See the GitHub-sync follow-on epic (linked discovered_from).

## Format

**NDJSON — one complete ticket object per line.** Serves reporting (row-oriented, stream-parseable) and migration (each line re-imports as a full ticket) from one artifact. Each line carries `schema_version`; run metadata (`exported_at`, `source_env`, counts) goes to stderr/log so every stdout line is a clean ticket.

## Export — implementation detail

- **Streaming (perf):** iterate ticket ids → `reduce_ticket(id)` → write one NDJSON line → release. Do NOT use `reduce_all_tickets` (it materializes every state in memory; `reducer/_api.py:179-245`). Per-ticket cache + SNAPSHOT bounding keep replay cheap; recommend pre-compact before a large export.
- `--strip-external` (alias `--no-jira`): **provider-neutral** stripping of all external-tracker linkage — `jira_comment_id` on comments, `bridge_alerts`, `SYNC` events, and any future provider id. This is the only seam the GitHub-sync follow-on inherits; no reconciler coupling.
- **Scope defaults (confirmed):** all work types, all statuses incl. closed; **exclude `session_log`** (`--include-session-logs`); **include archived** with an `archived:true` marker (`--exclude-archived`); **exclude deleted** (`--include-deleted`). Filters: `--status`, `--type`, `--parent`. Output: stdout default, `-o <file>`.

## Import — implementation detail

- Consumes our own NDJSON. Composes events through the **normal locked write path** (no raw-event injection): `CREATE` (+ `STATUS` to reach closed/in_progress — fills the gap where CREATE only yields `open`; + `COMMENT`/`LINK`/`TAG`/file-impact/verify-commands).
- **Provenance, not raw fidelity:** fresh ids + fresh HLC timestamps (HLC stays monotonic — never inject foreign timestamps). Each ticket carries `source_id`, `source_created_at`, `source_author`, `source_env`; comments carry `source_author`/`source_created_at`. The reducer's CREATE/COMMENT processors read these optional fields and surface them in `ticket_state` (additive/forward-compat — verify whether a SCHEMA_VERSION note is warranted; no new event types).
- **Idempotent skip by `source_id`:** one streaming scan of the target at start builds `{source_id → local_id}`; seen → skip, new → create. Re-runs / resume-after-crash never duplicate; existing tickets are never updated (that is sync = reconciler, out of scope).
- **Two-pass:** pass 1 create all tickets (capture id-map); pass 2 set parent (EDIT), links, status (STATUS), comments, tags. Dangling parent/link target not in the import set → skip + warn, never fail the run. Dedup links by `(source,target,relation)` (both endpoints list the dep). Blocking-link promotion re-runs deterministically (round-trip stable).
- `--dry-run`: report create/skip counts without writing.
- **Performance:** import runs with push deferred (`REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=off`) and pushes once at the end — eliminates per-event network round-trips. KNOWN LIMITATION (accepted): one git commit + one lock cycle per event (no batch primitive); a multi-thousand-event import takes several minutes and serializes the write lock (`_store/event_append.py:82-146`, `_store/lock.py:187-231`). A batch-commit primitive is deferred to a follow-up task. Document: pre-compact the source and set `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=off` for large imports.

## Surface

**CLI + library only (no MCP tools):** `rebar export` / `rebar import`; `rebar.export_tickets()` / `rebar.import_tickets()`. New `src/rebar/_io/`: `export_ndjson.py`, `import_ndjson.py`, `_provenance.py`, `_strip.py`. `schemas/export.schema.json` documents the per-line shape.

## Success Criteria

- `rebar export` emits schema-valid NDJSON; importing into a fresh repo reproduces the same logical state (status, assignee, links, tags, comments), modulo new ids and with original date/author preserved as `source_*` fields.
- Re-running an import (or resuming a killed one) produces zero duplicates.
- `--strip-external` leaves no external-tracker linkage in the output.
- Non-open statuses (closed/in_progress) are reproduced on import.
- Export streams (bounded memory); import defers push and pushes once at the end.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] NDJSON `export` (streaming via `reduce_ticket`) + per-line `schema_version` + scope flags + provider-neutral `--strip-external`/`--no-jira` + `schemas/export.schema.json`
- [ ] `import` composes events through the locked path (CREATE + STATUS + COMMENT/LINK/TAG), two-pass, `--dry-run`
- [ ] provenance fields (`source_id`/`source_created_at`/`source_author`/`source_env`) carried + surfaced in `ticket_state`; STATUS reproduction of non-open states
- [ ] idempotent skip by `source_id` (streaming target scan); existing tickets never updated
- [ ] import defers push (`REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=off`) + single final push; large-import limitation documented
- [ ] `src/rebar/_io/` adapters: export_ndjson, import_ndjson, _provenance, _strip
- [ ] tests: round-trip ≡ logical state; idempotent re-run (0 dups); strip-external; status reproduction; dangling-link skip; provenance surfacing; streaming-memory sanity on a large synthetic store
- [ ] DROPPED from original epic: MCP `export_tickets`/`import_tickets` + outputSchema (CLI/library only)

See `docs/remediation-implementation-plan.md` (P1.2). Refined by design brainstorm 2026-06-17.
compiled_state.title: P1.2 — rebar export / import (JSON snapshot + GitHub-issues ingest)
